chunks.text: works through  three types of people.  Imagine three people.  One is client, one is server.  Who is client?

chunks.text: and parsed it and showed me this screen.  This work happened so fast  because my internet.com  internet speed is very fast.  If it is so low, then people's speed is

chunks.text: even faster. But still  according to my locality, it is very fast.  So with this fast internet speed  all these things are  very fast. So the client

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: sends a request to the server through the browser.  Browser is the client's, that is, my assistant.  Whatever I say from the browser, he will do that.  It will help me to bring  this web page from the server.

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: Who is managing the server? The server is being managed  by youtube. They are managing the server  of youtube.com and  some code is written inside that server  that sends me this kind of code.

chunks.text: That is, when I write youtube.com,  this code came. Now how to convert this code  into this, this work is of the browser.  That is, how to show me, this work is of the browser.  So the client says something like this to the browser

chunks.text: Hey, give me youtube.com  Browser says, son,  do you want youtube.com? Wait, I'll ask  youtube.com for youtube.com.  Then the browser goes to the server, that is,

chunks.text: it sends a request to the server. It says to the server  Give me youtube.com. The server  will give it here. Here it will say  No, here it will say error.  Here it will say that the server has been errored.

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: Whatever the response will be, ideally  it should be 200. There is a response code  200. With that response,  our browser  comes back to the client

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: and shows this kind of page.  I hope this is clear to you. Client, browser,  server. Okay, so the client  goes to the browser, the browser goes to the server,  back to the browser, to the client. So this is how

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: this thing works. This thing is a little more important.  I'll tell you once again. When you write google.com,  any website, google.com, then the client  asks the browser to bring me google.com.  The browser says, O Gurudev,

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: you want google.com, wait,  I'll bring it from the server. So the browser goes to the server  and brings google.com  and gives it to you. You  get google.com. Now along with google.com,

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: the server sends all  images, all videos,  whatever things are there, it sends all of them. Like these icons,  it sends all of this. The URL of these icons  is also written in this code.

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: And that too, the job of the browser is to  display it properly. So the server  sends a whole bundle to the browser.  It sends a bundle like this. And by passing this bundle,  showing it like this

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: is the job of the browser. Now further  whatever interaction the user will do with this page,  the browser will ask for additional requests.  If I do it rightly and inspect it,  and then I go to the network, and here

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: I write, let's say, Harry bhai. Okay,  let's say I wrote Harry bhai. So see, he sent so many  requests for images,  he sent a lot of other requests. So as you  interact, you will have to send

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: additional requests to the browser.  So in total, you can consider it like a  restaurant. You gave an order,  that is, you sent a request. As soon as you  gave the order, a waiter

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: took your order. The waiter then went inside the  kitchen. He sent your order to the kitchen.  Consider the kitchen as a server. And  finally, when the kitchen gave the order  back, what did this

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: waiter do, who is doing the job of the browser?  That waiter gave  you the order back.  It is possible that the order cannot be made in the kitchen  for some reason. It is very rare that you

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: order something and you do not get it. But it happens  sometimes. Similarly, it happens here too.  You get to see this error. 404 error  or 500 error. The server  is not working. Such errors.

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: So whenever such errors are seen,  you are told. But  ideally, 200 means okay  response comes. And you get  such a webpage. So you can also consider it

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: like a restaurant. So whenever you write  google.com slash mail, then if google.com  slash mail is available, it will show you. And if you  write codewithari.com slash contact, then  codewithari.com slash contact if available

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: will show you. If not available, then 404  will show you. Or if there is a 500 error, then it will show  you a 500 page. Whatever server  will send, it will show you. So this cycle  keeps going. The client sent the request.

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: The browser then sent the server.  The server then processed the  request. After this, the server  sent the data back to the browser.  And whatever data was sent, the browser showed it to the client.

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: Then the client did something.  The browser sent the same request to the server.  Then the server sent something. Whatever data  the server sent, it came back to the client through the browser.  And so on.

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: So now you will say that all this is fine. But  what is this backend? What is the meaning of backend?  Backend is how the server  is processing the request. Now  if you have a blog.

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: There is a lot of data in the blog.  So what will you do?  Will you store every webpage of the blog separately?  Or will you generate that content dynamically?  You will generate that content dynamically.

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: I take the example of google.  If I search for Harry  then you can see that the  header of google, these links  this format does not change at all.

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: If I write here, let's say  Shubham.  Let's say I wrote Shubham here.  Now see  when I wrote Harry,

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: its content is showing me very similar content.  So what does google do? The backend  of the google server  processes the data in such a way  that it can send this kind of

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: HTML for the corresponding query.  That is, when I write Shubham, it sends this.  When I write Harry,  it sends this. So the process of generating  this thing

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: is happening through the system.  Now this backend can be Django.  This backend can be Flask.  This backend can be NodeJS. This backend  can be PHP. This backend can be

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: Ruby on Rails. And the database  inside the backend is used.  That is, the backend code is not visible to the client.  This is the front-end code that the server  sends. And how the server made this code,

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: we don't know. Because this code is  running on the server. If you are getting confused,  then don't get confused at all. You will understand all these things.  We will see the backend later.  Now we understand that

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: how the front-end works. Now the  front-end is primarily  made up of three languages.  HTML, CSS and JavaScript.  These are the three languages that

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: are needed if you are making a website.  Every website needs HTML, CSS,  JavaScript in most of the cases.  If you only use HTML,  then it becomes a very basic skeleton

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: of the website.  After that, when you use CSS,  you can style it. Like this button  looks like this. So it is only visible  because of CSS. Along with that,

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
